zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret St. Quintin於大約1362年出生在英國越可爾宏比 卡素時，她的父親John de St. Quintin28歲，母親Elizabeth de Swinhoe23歲。她於大約1391年與Sir John Conyers在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1426年10月在英國英格蘭越可爾宏比享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret St. Quintin于大约1362年出生在英国越可爾宏比 卡素时，她的父亲John de St. Quintin28岁，母亲Elizabeth de Swinhoe23岁。她于大约1391年与Sir John Conyers在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1426年10月在英国英格兰越可爾宏比享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: St
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Quintin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Yves, Andre, Dominique, Gilles, Lucien, Michel, Oliva, Roch.
Breton: from the personal name Quintin, which is of the same Latin origin as 2 below, but also considered a derivative of Old Breton kint ‘first’.
French; Spanish (Quintín): from the personal name Quintin (Spanish Quintín), from Latin Quintinus, a Christian saint's name (see Quentin ).

variants.name: Quinton
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Quentin, Quintin (Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, and brought to England by the Normans. The surname was altered to Quinton by association with the placenames in 2 below.

English: habitational name from from any of three places called Quinton (Gloucestershire, Northamptonshire, Worcestershire) or from Quainton (Buckinghamshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English cwēn ‘queen’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, or perhaps from Old English cwene ‘woman’ + tūn or an Old English personal name Cwēna + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Quentin (Manche) in France or possibly from Saint-Quentin-en-Tourmont (Somme), the site of the martyrdom of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. It is not certain that this surname survived into the modern period.

variants.name: Quentin
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Quentin (from Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. Compare Quintin .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret St. Quintin was born about 1362, in Hornby Castle, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de St. Quintin, was 28 and her mother, Elizabeth de Swinhoe, was 23. She married Sir John Conyers about 1391, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in October 1426, in Hornby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret St. Quintin est née environ 1362, à Hornby Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de St. Quintin, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Swinhoe, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Conyers environ 1391, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en octobre 1426, à Hornby, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret St. Quintinが約1362年にイギリスヨークシュアイアホールンビー カストルで生まれた時，父John de St. Quintinは28歳，母Elizabeth de Swinhoeは23歳であった。約1391年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir John Conyersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1426年10月にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールンビーで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret St. Quintin(이)가 대략 1362년 영국 요르크실르 호른비 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de St. Quintin(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Swinhoe(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1391년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir John Conyers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1426년 10월 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 호른비에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret St. Quintin родилась около 1362 г. в местности хаурнби кастл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de St. Quintin было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Swinhoe, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Conyers около 1391 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла октябрь 1426 г. в местности хаурнби, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.
